base.$description: BASE tier — raw, primitive values. First tier of the standard DTCG base/semantic/component convention. Read with usesDtcg:true. path[0] is always 'base'. COLOR: $value is the OKLCH interchange spine as a literal string; never pre-convert to hex. Other spaces live in $extensions.brand.spaces, each flagged source 'derived' (auto-generated from OKLCH) or source 'authored' (a value the brand DECLARES as truth, e.g. a spot/CMYK print color, which must NOT be re-derived from OKLCH because spot colors do not round-trip). category names at path[1] follow the namespace convention (color, spacing, font-size, font-family, font-weight, line-height, border-radius, shadow). NOTE: these are illustrative example values — replace them with the brand's own. Plain prose is used instead of brace-placeholders here because the curly-brace syntax is reserved for DTCG aliases.

brand-2.$description: accent ink (example showing an AUTHORED print value)

spacing.$description: base grid — replace the scale to match the brand

shadow.$description: Plain-string composite values (not objects) for lossless ingestion. Tint in a brand neutral, not pure black. OPTIONAL dimension — delete if the brand is flat.

motion.$description: OPTIONAL dimension (carried DTCG-valid even where no current consumer ingests it). Delete if the brand does not move.
